plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-559 RP18.P03.M02.S15-RP18.P03.M04.S12 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-559 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18.P03 implementation workflow slice: the M02 Type And Shape Definition tail (the six remaining rows), the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (the first twelve rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-560 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-559 (RP18.P03.M02.S15-RP18.P03.M04.S12, risk B, 40 units) is a clean descriptor-only AI legal workflows pack. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms full internal consistency across registry.js (CP559 pack binding), service.js (freezeCp559Result + case-set/descriptor factories, lines 1057-1176), validators.js (coverage/descriptor/Hermes/Claude/handoff factories, lines 1566-1733), contract JSON (current_pack, p03_implementation_workflow_slice_descriptor, cp559_requirements, cp559_no_write_attestation, historical bindings), the validate-rp18 script (deepEqual contract-drift checks), README CP00-559 entry, closeout-pack-plan.json entry, manifest, and command-evidence. Deliverable distribution (claude_review:2, implementation:19, contract:9, security_audit:4, test:5, hermes_evidence:1) and micro-title distribution (6/22/12) reconcile to 40 everywhere. Three CP559 tests (model.test.js:586-636) cover coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries. command-evidence.json records all 27 validation commands at exit_code 0 (node --check/--test, rp18 validate, npm test/validate/build, git diff --check). The production_ready claim is honest: manifest reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor; the production_ready_candidate=true flag is strictly a descriptor-validation result (coverage.valid && slice.valid), distinct from runtime gates, so no production_ready gate is weakened. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are comprehensively attested (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). Claude remains a read-only non-final reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false; human final approval required, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation). Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic review packet/receipt handling, not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening holds: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON ok:true, non-zero stdout, well-formed, no fenced/tool-shaped output), tools restricted to Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools, read_only=true, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used (summary-only prohibited and not used), prompt 2253B under the 25600B limit, review schema present. Changed-file-scope hygiene is correct: active implementation refs scoped to the RP18 package/contract/validator; unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, workbook/matter docs, ldip-integration) preserved and not inferred into the pack; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. No P0-P3 findings.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-559 ai legal workflows core p03 implementation workflow slice descriptor pack
